text: Although Africa has not contributed significantly to the green house gases that cause climate change, this civilisation is being severely affected by human-induced climate change in almost all areas of development. This devastating impact is occurring across the entire continent with very high levels of confidence, and is being experienced as increased loss of biodiversity, increased frequencies of droughts and decreases in crop yields. Most ominously of all, climate change is resulting in a real loss of life and it has been estimated that it will stunt the economic growth of the poorest countries on the continent by as much as 5%. This creates the most unjust imbalance of any environmental problem in the world where it is confirmed that those who have the least to do with causing the effect, suffer the most and have no capacity to afford to adapt.
